claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're claiming for.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my three children. 

I have Alex, who is 5 years old, Blake, who is 17, and Charlie, who is 19.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me gather a bit more detail about each of your children.

First, I need to understand their living and education situations:

**Does Alex (age 5) live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you!

**Does Blake (age 17) live with you, and are they currently in full-time education or on an approved training course?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake lives with me, but no, they're not in education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying.

**Is Blake registered with their local careers service, Connexions, or a similar organisation for training or work experience? Or are they perhaps signed up with the armed forces?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake hasn't registered with careers service or anything like that. They just finished school and aren't doing anything at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Blake.

Now, let me ask about your eldest child:

**Is Charlie (age 19) in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or on an approved training course?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is still in education. They're doing A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just to clarify regarding Charlie:

**Does Charlie live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie lives with me as well. All three of them are at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

I have one more question about Charlie. Since Child Benefit for children in education can continue until a certain point:

**When did Charlie turn 19, and when are they expected to finish their A-levels?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
I need to provide accurate information here. According to my situation, Charlie is 19 and in approved education, but I don't have specific details about when they turned 19 or when their course finishes. 

Charlie turned 19 earlier this year and they're expected to finish their A-levels at the end of this academic year, so around June or July.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me also check the guidance about children who are 16 or over to get more specific details:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your three children.

Before I provide my assessment, I have one final question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your three children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, here is my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

## Summary

You are **eligible for Child Benefit for 2 out of your 3 children**.

---

### ✅ Alex (age 5) — **Eligible**
Alex is under 16 and lives with you. You live in the UK and are responsible for them. All the basic eligibility criteria are met.

---

### ❌ Blake (age 17) — **Not Eligible**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Since Blake is over 16, they would need to be in approved education or training to qualify. Blake is currently not in education or training, and has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces.

**However**, if Blake registers with a careers service (such as Connexions or similar) or the armed forces, you may be able to claim Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks. This might be worth considering if Blake is looking for work or training opportunities.

---

### ✅ Charlie (age 19) — **Eligible**
Charlie qualifies because they are under 20 and currently in approved education (A-levels count as approved education). Please note that your eligibility for Charlie will end when they finish their A-levels (around June/July) or if they turn 20, whichever comes first.

---

If you have any further questions or if Blake's circumstances change, please don't hesitate to contact the Child Benefit Office for guidance. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with you, meaning you are responsible for them. You live in the UK. All the basic eligibility criteria are met. You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 17 years old, which means they fall into the '16 or over' category. For children aged 16-19, you can normally still qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is not currently in full-time education or on an approved training course. Additionally, Blake has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service (such as Connexions) or the armed forces, which would have allowed you to claim Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks after leaving education. Since Blake is over 16, not in approved education or training, and not registered with a careers service or armed forces, you are NOT eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 19 years old and is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The rules state you can normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. A-levels are considered approved education. Charlie lives with you. Since Charlie is under 20, in approved education, and lives with you, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie. Note: Your eligibility will end when Charlie finishes their A-levels (around June/July) or turns 20, whichever comes first.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for two of your three children. Alex (age 5) qualifies as a child under 16 living with you. Charlie (age 19) qualifies because they are under 20 and in approved education (A-levels). However, Blake (age 17) does not qualify because they are over 16, not in approved education or training, and have not registered with a careers service or the armed forces.
